message:
fix(db): reset circuit breaker on first-attempt success after prior failures

The retry_on_db_lock decorator only reset the circuit breaker when
attempt > 0 (i.e., after retries). This meant a first-attempt success
after the circuit entered HALF_OPEN state wouldn't reset the failure
counter. The circuit would stay degraded until the timeout expired
naturally, even though operations were succeeding.

Now checks _circuit_breaker_failures > 0 instead, so any success while
the breaker has accumulated failures resets it immediately.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -263,9 +263,9 @@ async def wrapper(*args: Any, **kwargs: Any) -> Any:
             for attempt in range(max_retries + 1):
                 try:
                     result = await func(*args, **kwargs)
-                    # Success - reset circuit breaker
-                    if use_circuit_breaker and attempt > 0:
-                        # Only record success if we had retries (indicates recovery)
+                    # Success - reset circuit breaker if it had any accumulated failures,
+                    # even on the first attempt (allows recovery from HALF_OPEN state).
+                    if use_circuit_breaker and _circuit_breaker_failures > 0:
                         await _record_circuit_success()
                     return result
